_meta.title: Daily RevOps Report — canonical source-per-metric registry
_meta.purpose: The SINGLE machine-readable map of metric -> {source type, exact id, tab, desk/owner, dedup key, reconciliation anchor} for the Daily RevOps Report. scripts/render_daily.py resolves its human-managed / drift-prone sources (Pre-IPO desks, booking sheet, sendivo sub-accounts, workspace roster) FROM this file, so registry = code = reality (no comment-vs-code drift). A chat building or QAing the report reads THIS to answer 'am I sourcing the right thing for this metric?' WITHOUT a human hand-feeding it. Run `render_daily.py <date> --verify` to confirm every source is live, correctly-shaped, and (for Pre-IPO) reconciles to the team tally.
_meta.cutover_2026_06_30: ALL meetings (§1/§2/§3/§5) moved from the Funding-Form 'Data' sheet -> the portal main.raw_im_bookings (the sheet froze after 06-29 and silently zeroed 06-30 meetings). §1b EMAIL-KPIs-BY-INFRA removed (layout now = June-26 gold). §2 gained the Renaissance 3 (webform, sub 14603) SMS row. Ref handoffs/2026-06-30-BUILD-daily-report-v3-jun30-complete-jun29-repair.md.

schema.source_type: sheet | warehouse | api  — where the metric is read from
schema.sheet: { spreadsheet_id, tab, date_column, dedup_key, owner } (Google Sheet)
schema.warehouse: { relation, key_columns } (DuckDB serving snapshot via read API)
schema.dedup_key: the field(s) collapsed to one booking, e.g. 'email|phone' = lower(email) else phone
schema.reconciliation: { type, source, pattern, rule } — the independent tally a render reconciles to so drift is flagged, not silently rendered (NULL = structural health-gate only)

_meta.change_capture: When a desk/sheet is ADDED, RENAMED, or a sub-account/key changes: update THIS file in the same PR that touches the renderer (or before). Never let the report discover drift by Sam eyeballing a wrong number. Pre-IPO desks specifically: the authoritative desk roster is owned by the #pre-ipo-success team (see open_questions[0]); add a desk here the moment it appears in that channel.
_meta.self_verify_protocol: A QA/build chat confirms a metric with zero hand-feeding by: (1) reading this registry for the metric's declared source(s); (2) running `render_daily.py <date> --verify` which probes each source is reachable + has its declared columns + parses non-degenerate, and reconciles Pre-IPO per-desk counts to the anchor; (3) any DRIFT / UNREACHABLE / column-missing prints LOUDLY and the verify exits non-zero (drift is flagged, never silently rendered).

workspaces._note: Workspace identity is shared across §1 Instantly key slug, §4 account_label.workspace_slug, §6 email_message.workspace_id, and core.workspace.slug — ONE slug end-to-end. Render order = list order. renaissance-2 is Funding 5 (Eyver) for EMAIL; it is ALSO the SMS Pre-IPO sub-account (13922) — do not conflate the two channels.

email_sent_opps.what: Email sent + opportunities per workspace. This ONE fetch also feeds §4 'Actual' (so §1 sent and §4 Actual can never disagree).
wrong_sources: pipeline raw_pipeline_campaign_daily_metrics drops 140k-270k sends/day into a NULL bucket (Funding 5 read 91k vs real 232k)

reconciliation.rule: a REPORTING workspace absent from the API result renders 0 sent/opps (wrong-but-plausible zero, e.g. dead/rotated key) -> WARN loudly, never silent.

email_meetings_leadtype.section: §1 Meetings + Cheap/Regular split (and the booking universe for §2/§3/§5)
email_meetings_leadtype.what: ALL meetings (§1 email by workspace, §2 SMS+WhatsApp, §3 Call, §5 by partner) + cheap-vs-regular MCA split. ONE booking source, deduped email|phone, split by `channel`.

warehouse.filter: offer='Funding', latest _snapshot_date, non-deleted; channel -> §1 Email / §2 SMS+WhatsApp / §3 Call; workspace -> §1 by-workspace via ws_alias(); partner -> §5
warehouse.date_column: date  (ET BUSINESS date, a clean 'YYYY-MM-DD' string — NOT created_at, a UTC ingest/reseed timestamp that splits ~28 rows across UTC-midnight into the wrong day [Sam 06-30])
warehouse.dedup_key: email|phone  (one booking per person; `id` churns on Darcy reseeds, never key on it)
warehouse.leadtype_rule: cheap = lead_type='cheap'; regular = any other non-empty lead_type

email_meetings_leadtype.cutover: 2026-06-30 — was the Funding-Form 'Data' sheet below; that sheet froze/retired after 06-29 (0 rows for 06-30), which silently zeroed every meeting column. im_bookings is the live portal writer (Darcy's form). Ref reference_renaissance_portal_supabase_booking_source_20260629 / reference_portal_im_bookings_darcy_form_live_writer_20260630.

sheet.status: RETIRED as the renderer's booking source 2026-06-30 (kept here only as the legacy Funding-Form reconciliation target until portal-vs-FF tallies 100%). NOT read by render_daily anymore.

wrong_sources: sendivo delivery_metrics returns the DEFAULT account (~178) regardless of sub_account_id — NOT the SMS-sent source
the Sendivo dashboard/UI: BLAST-ONLY and hides AIM API sends — never expect it to equal §2 Sent (reconcile via the §2 blast-split line)
sms_sent.inclusive_basis: RATIFIED [Sam 07-02, TKT-3 REVERSED]: §2 Sent = the day's TOTAL messages — blasts + follow-ups + AIM conversational (we pay for every message equally; billing quantity is exactly this total, verified Jul-1 Ren3: 71,474 blast + 4,520 other = 75,992 billed). The fix lives in the SYNC/renderer, never a hand-edit.
sms_sent.delivered_failed: §2 SMS Delivered + Fail% = warehouse main.v_sms_campaign_performance per sub_account_name for the day — per-message CARRIER status from the nightly /sms/logs pull (statuses final at pull time; verified to reconcile the Sendivo dashboard delivery %). D-1: no/partial view row -> Delivered/Fail render '—' + WARN and back-fill at the 12:45Z re-render [2026-07-03 doctrine — the old delivered≈sent fallback was a guessed value, removed]. Sendivo '# contacts − SMS sent' = skipped at send, NOT undelivered. Real-time outbound-status webhook dead since 06-09 (vendor restore ask open).

sms_blast_reconcile.what: Blast-only vs follow-up/AIM split per sub-account for the day, so §2's inclusive Sent reconciles to the Sendivo dashboard's blast-only view (TKT-3 REVERSED [Sam 07-02]). blast_id populated on real sends since 2026-06-26.

warehouse.rule: blast_id IS NOT NULL = blasts (dashboard basis); NULL = follow-ups/nudges + AIM conversational + system. D-1 (nightly /sms/logs) -> 'pending' until loaded.

sms_opps_daily.what: Daily OPPORTUNITIES stage of the ratified funnel = Qwen positive-intent verdicts over the day's human (non-opt-out) replies, attributed to the sub-account via the inbound receipt's own sub_account_name. The ONLY SMS opp signal — v_sms_campaign_performance.positive_replies is a BANNED misnomer (ALL non-opt-out replies, ~8x inflation) and must never be rendered.

warehouse.honest_lag_gate: Qwen runs ~2-3d behind (09:00Z incremental -> seed -> next nightly): a sub's day renders ONLY when >=90% of its human replies carry a verdict, else '—' (100%-or-wipe); heals on the D-2/D-3 backfill re-renders. WhatsApp replies have NO classifier -> WA Opps always '—' (gap-listed).

r3_webform_funnel.what: Ren3's canonical web-form funnel for the ET day: repliers -> warm positives -> app links -> form fills.

api.endpoint: SELECT ... FROM comms.v_r3_daily_funnel WHERE et_day = <report day>

wrong_sources: comms.message (drops suppressed STOP inbounds since 07-01, ~34% of raw — reply/opt-out totals must read webhook_receipt-based sources)

sms_cost_daily.what: Actual all-in Sendivo $ (total_spend) per sub-account per day [DR-3 basis]. Cost/mtg·form = day cost ÷ that row's Mtgs/Webform; '—' when the unit count is 0. WhatsApp = MODEL $0.072 × delivered (paid on delivery only; NO Iskra billing feed exists — rendered WITH an explicit model label [Sam 07-02], never summed into the SMS actual total). NO client-facing price concept exists (the older client-facing $0.07×delivered framing is DEAD). Doctrine flat rates for per-blast/margin math: SMS $0.0072/msg paid sent-or-not · WA $0.072/msg paid on delivery.

warehouse.sanity: 2026-06-30 sub 12720 sms_fee_usd = 346.03 (verified exact vs live billing API)
warehouse.freshness_guard: nightly 'sendivo billing_daily' upsert (7d window) — an evening render's same-day row can be a PARTIAL-day snapshot; sms_fee_qty diverging >5% from the LIVE billed quantity (= §2 Sent) -> cost renders '—' + loud WARN and back-fills post-nightly (100%-or-wipe).

sms_replies_total.what: TOTAL inbound SMS replies (incl. opt-outs) + opt-out count per sub-account [doctrine 07-02: the funnel stage is TOTAL replies; there is NO positive-reply metric for SMS/WA]. Source basis = comms.webhook_receipt sendivo_inbound receipts (raw_sendivo_inbound mirrors them) — NOT comms.message, which drops suppressed STOP inbounds (~34% of raw since 07-01).

sms_kpi_to_opp.section: (NOT RENDERED — the trailing-window §2b was removed from the daily tab 2026-07-02 [DR-10]; §2b is now a same-day opt-outs·KPI·cost table. This entry + view stay for other consumers/--dry.)
sms_kpi_to_opp.what: SMS sent / opps / sent-per-opp per workspace (Ren1 Funding / Ren2 Pre-IPO / Ren3 webform). Sent/opp is THE number gating the web-form-vs-meeting economics decision (Ido: '~5,000 and web apps don't pencil'). opp = positive-intent inbound SMS reply = the CURRENT-native notion derived.sms_reply_is_positive_qwen.is_positive (do NOT build a bespoke reclassifier [Sam 2026-06-30]).

warehouse.opp_definition: opps = count of Qwen positive-intent replies (derived.sms_reply_is_positive_qwen where is_positive) attributed to the sub-account by reply number; sent from v_sms_campaign_performance; sent_per_opp = sent/opps.
warehouse.cost: Cost $ (window) + Cost/opp [backlog A2, added 2026-07-01] = the funnel's cost_usd (rollup of v_sms_campaign_performance.cost_usd — the Sendivo CAMPAIGN-FEED per-message $) summed over the SAME window dates ÷ the SAME opps — window-consistent by construction. NB deliberately a DIFFERENT cost basis than §2's billing sms_fee_usd (feed vs billing, window vs day); both labeled. NB2 the backlog's June 'proven' $0.79/$1.03/$0.44 divided by v_sms_campaign_performance.positive_replies, which that view defines as ALL non-opt-out replies (a misnomer, NOT positive-intent) — the rendered Cost/opp divides by the Qwen opps in the adjacent column instead, so it reads ~6x higher and is internally consistent.
warehouse.window: TRAILING fully-classified window: the most-recent up-to-7 SMS-reply days (<= report date) where >= 90% of that day's human (non-opt-out) inbound carries a Qwen verdict. Same-day/recent opps are NOT shown live.
warehouse.why_trailing: The Qwen classifier runs on a ~2-3d lag (cron /root/sms-sentiment-bi/incremental.sh @09:00 UTC -> seed -> loaded next nightly), so the render day's opps are unclassified at 10pm-ET report time. A partial classification massively UNDER-counts opps (06-29 read 4% classified) and would poison the KPI-to-opp gate — hence a fully-classified trailing window + 'pending' for same-day (feedback_partial_data_100pct_or_wipe_20260614). This is why §2 historically carried NO opp column.
warehouse.ren3_caveat: Ren3 (webform) sends route via the AIM API and are NOT in the campaign feed -> its funnel sent is 0 -> Sent/opp renders '—' (opps still shown).

sms_campaign_leaderboard.section: §2c SMS campaign leaderboard — REMOVED from the report 2026-07-01 (Jun-30 accuracy pass); the view main.v_sms_campaign_performance stays and now feeds §2 delivered/failed. Entry kept for the attribution intel below.
sms_campaign_leaderboard.what: Per-campaign sent -> opp ranking over the same trailing fully-classified window, best converters first (lowest sent/opp), campaigns with >= 10 in-window opps. NOT rendered since 2026-07-01.

warehouse.relation: main.v_sms_campaign_performance (sent) + derived.sms_reply_is_positive_qwen (opps) attributed via main.v_sendivo_number_campaign (our_number -> campaign)
warehouse.grain: CAMPAIGN — reply number -> campaign is 1:1 (0 numbers map to >1 campaign) and maps 100% of opps.
warehouse.grain_cap: Per-BLAST/script attribution is NOT clean: the outbound-message log (main.v_sendivo_outbound_message) ties only ~22% of positive opps to a blast (log is sparse — only ~30% of reply phones covered, recent days only). The real per-script fix needs Sendivo blast_id on the won-export — BLOCKED on Larry/Sendivo (said yes, untested). Do NOT ship a per-blast leaderboard until that lands. Also do NOT ship SMS sent/MEETING per-campaign — SMS meetings carry NULL campaign_id (only ~7 mapped) -> fake infinite/zero KPIs.

whatsapp.what: WhatsApp sent / delivered / failed / replies (ISKRA 'sent' runs ~30-37% failed; KPI = delivered/mtg).
whatsapp.meetings_source: main.raw_im_bookings, channel == 'WhatsApp' [cutover 2026-06-30; was the Funding-Form sheet]
whatsapp.cost_model: MODEL $0.072 × delivered [Sam 07-02, FINAL: WA is paid ONLY on delivery] — no Iskra billing feed exists (raw cost 100% NULL); labelled 'model' in §2b, excluded from the SMS actual TOTAL. Vendor ask (Arseny): a real Iskra billing/cost feed.
whatsapp.opps: NO Qwen classifier over Iskra/WA replies yet -> §2 WA Opps renders '—' (gap-listed; internal build).

wa_preipo_meetings.what: WhatsApp Pre-IPO meetings for the yellow summary block — was HARDCODED 0 until 2026-07-02 (registry DR-9); now read from the portal like every other booking metric (wa_preipo_meetings in render_daily.py). Sent stays 0: no WA Pre-IPO send feed exists (v_sms_dash_wa_daily is the Funding ISKRA line). The adjacent SEC 125 / Tariffs / R&D Credit rows STAY hardcoded 0 — those offers do not exist yet anywhere (no booking source, no send feed).

preipo_meetings.section: §2 Ren2 (SMS · Pre-IPO) meetings + yellow block 'SMS IPO'
preipo_meetings.what: Pre-IPO ('SMS IPO') meetings = the sum of bookings across the active Pre-IPO booking DESKS. THIS is the metric that the data ticket was opened for: the desks were not enumerated/labeled in a way a chat could confirm, and a human had to hand-feed 'Collins + Summit'.
preipo_meetings.additive_rule: A lead books ONE desk or the other (Collins overflow -> Summit, per Grace). Desks are ADDITIVE; count each desk independently and SUM. Do NOT cross-dedup between desks. Dedup ONLY within each desk by email|phone.
preipo_meetings.count_rule: Count rows whose 'Date' (booking-made date) == report day, deduped within the desk by email|phone.

desks.owner: Collins desk (advisor names per the sheet: Tsvi Bort / Lawrence Michelson — NOT stated in Slack, treat as sheet-internal)
desks.label_provenance: Grace -> Sam DM 2026-06-29 explicitly maps 1IZzmCXt… = collins; Ido posted this sheet in #pre-ipo-success 2026-04-27.

Grace -> Sam DM 2026-06-29 explicitly maps 1oKlY_2qI… = summit; Ido posted it in #pre-ipo-chat 2026-06-10. The earlier 'Craig Diana' label was WRONG ('Craig' was only an advisor first-name at setup); do NOT re-mislabel this sheet.

wrong_sources: the consolidated 1vaExhxu sheet does NOT carry the Pre-IPO desks

known_good.source: #pre-ipo-success per-desk counter (Jamil Matias run, peaked Collins 23 / Summit 11); matches both sheets.

reconciliation.line_regex_note: Desk-AGNOSTIC on purpose: it captures '<Word> Booked <N>' at the start of a line, so a NEW desk posting in the channel (the way 'Summit' was once new + uncaptured) is detected as an unknown-desk drift signal. Known vs unknown is decided against desks[] in code, not baked into the regex. Anchored to line-start AND the count must be followed by a non-letter (separator/end-of-line) via negative lookahead — so a freeform sentence line like 'Summit Booked 99 leads in pipeline' or 'We Booked 3 meetings' does NOT corrupt a desk's counter.
reconciliation.rule: Each desk posts a running counter that RESETS to 1 each ET calendar day, and EACH BOOKER runs their own independent 1..N. So take MAX N per (desk, booker) within the report ET day (by message POST date — NOT the 'June DD' script-name in the text), then SUM across bookers per desk (two bookers each reaching 11 = 22, not max=11; one booker's run split over messages still collapses to their single max). Reconcile that to the rendered per-desk sheet count. On mismatch FLAG drift. A desk label seen in the channel that is NOT in desks[] is itself drift (a new/renamed desk -> update this registry).
reconciliation.caveats: There is NO canonical desk list / pinned roster in Slack and NO end-of-day grand-total message — the per-booker counter is the only signal (see open_questions[0]). LIMITATION: this reads only top-level channel messages (Slack conversations.history), so if a desk ever posts its counter as a THREAD reply it would be missed and reconciliation would false-flag drift (the live 2026-06-29 run confirmed desks post top-level). Darcy is wiring an automated 'Renny' bot (~2026-06-30) to post all future bookings tagged with partner + channel; when it lands the line format may change and this regex/rule may need updating. In all cases the rendered Pre-IPO number comes from the desk SHEETS (source of truth) — this anchor only decides whether a drift alert fires.
reconciliation.degrade: If Slack creds/scopes are unavailable, --verify falls back to known_good when the verify date matches, else reports ANCHOR_UNAVAILABLE (explicit, not a pass). The structural health-gate (reachable + Date column present + non-degenerate parse) ALWAYS runs.

close_calls.what: Dials / distinct leads / connects (>=60s real convo, ET day). Close is the system of record; it UNDER-captures vs the warm-callers' own EOD tallies — note it, do not 'fix' it. PLUS warm-caller MTD rows [backlog A7 / Sam ask #5, added 2026-07-01]: dials / distinct people dialed / connects>=60s over 1st-of-report-month..report-day (same source + ET-day frame; June full-month proof 6,283/3,187/328).
close_calls.meetings_source: main.raw_im_bookings, channel == 'Call' [cutover 2026-06-30; was the Funding-Form sheet]

sending_truth_expected.what: Expected = sum of ACTIVE accounts' configured daily_limit per workspace x infra (OTD/Google; Outlook excluded), latest census, same-day (no lag).

warehouse.filter: lifecycle='Active' AND infra IN ('OTD','Google'), census_date = max

sending_truth_expected.actual_source: Instantly daily per workspace = the SAME fetch as metrics.email_sent_opps (so §4 Actual == §1 sent)
wrong_sources: raw_account_truth_daily_actuals lags 2-3d (the '150k on a June-29 tab' bug)

bookings_by_partner.what: Meetings booked grouped by partner (all channels).

warehouse.note: cutover 2026-06-30; was the Funding-Form 'Data' sheet (frozen after 06-29)

im_reply_time.what: First-reply latency per workspace (daily/weekly/monthly): first prospect reply (ue_type 2) -> first our reply (ue_type 3) per thread.
wrong_sources: raw_pipeline_conversation_messages chain froze iam_response_time / v_sla_reply_time_daily at 2026-06-23
im_reply_time.pending: SMS + WhatsApp first-reply time not yet computed (rendered 'pending')

open_questions.question: Is there a single canonical/authoritative list of ALL active Pre-IPO booking desks + their Google Sheet IDs (so a new desk like 'Summit' is captured automatically next time), or does each desk just have its own sheet known only ad-hoc in Slack? And will the team commit to updating that list (and this registry) when a desk is added/renamed? Specifically: will the new 'Renny' automation (Darcy, ~2026-06-30) become the authoritative, machine-readable booking source for Pre-IPO (with partner + channel + desk per booking) — and if so, should the renderer read Pre-IPO meetings FROM Renny's output instead of the two desk sheets?
open_questions.why: Confirmed 2026-06-30 via Slack: there is NO pinned roster/canvas/doc in #pre-ipo-success and NO auto-discoverable desk list — a newly-added desk only appears as a new '<Desk> Booked' label in the post stream, so it would silently drop out of the Pre-IPO total until Sam eyeballs a wrong number. This registry's preipo_meetings.desks must equal that roster. The Renny automation may make this whole sheet-scrape obsolete.
open_questions.status: OPEN — routed to Sam 2026-06-30 (to relay to Grace/Darcy); registered in reference_people_pending_asks_20260629
